content:
# Delivery Platform Audit Template

**Tool ID:** T103-01
**Block:** B103 (Delivery Channel Strategy)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Template
**Role:** Owner
**Time Required:** 30–45 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

Delivery platforms can be either a profitable revenue channel or a margin-destroying obligation — and most cafe owners do not know which one theirs is. This audit template forces you to look at the actual numbers: commissions, order volumes, average values, customer acquisition costs, and net profitability per platform. The answers may surprise you.

Fill this in with real data. Make decisions based on evidence, not assumptions.

**Built for Owner working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

A structured audit template for evaluating each delivery platform partnership on financial performance, operational impact, and strategic value. Produces a clear comparison across platforms to inform decisions about which to keep, renegotiate, or drop.

This tool supports **Delivery Channel Strategy** (B103) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Owner
**Time Required:** 30–45 minutes (with platform dashboards and POS data open)
**Frequency:** Quarterly. Also run the audit before any contract renewal or when considering a new platform.

**What You'll Need:**
- This template (printed or digital)
- Login access to each delivery platform's merchant dashboard
- POS data for the audit period (last 3 months recommended)
- Most recent commission/fee invoices from each platform
- Calculator or spreadsheet

**Steps:**
1. Complete one copy of Section 2 for each platform you use
2. Fill in financial data from platform dashboards and invoices
3. Calculate net margin per platform
4. Assess operational impact
5. Complete the comparison matrix in Section 4
6. Make strategic decisions based on the data

---

## Template

### Section 1: Audit Overview

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Date | *e.g., 15/03/2026* |
| Auditor | *e.g., Matas (Owner)* |
| Audit period | *e.g., Jan–Mar 2026 (Q1)* |
| Platforms currently active | *e.g., Wolt, Bolt Food* |
| Platforms under consideration | *e.g., Direct ordering (own website)* |

### Section 2: Platform Performance (complete one per platform)

**Platform name:** *e.g., Wolt*

#### Financial Performance

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Total orders (audit period) | *e.g., 340* | |
| Total gross revenue (EUR) | *e.g., 4,080* | *Before platform deductions* |
| Average order value (EUR) | *e.g., 12.00* | |
| Commission rate (%) | *e.g., 30%* | *Check contract — may vary by service* |
| Total commission paid (EUR) | *e.g., 1,224* | |
| Other platform fees (EUR) | *e.g., 85 (tablet rental, marketing)* | |
| Packaging cost for delivery orders (EUR) | *e.g., 170 (EUR 0.50 per order)* | |
| Net revenue after all deductions (EUR) | *e.g., 2,601* | |
| Food cost for delivery orders (EUR) | *e.g., 1,224 (30% COGS)* | |
| Labour cost allocated to delivery (EUR) | *e.g., 408 (10% of revenue)* | |
| **Net profit from this platform (EUR)** | *e.g., 969* | |
| **Net margin (%)** | *e.g., 23.8%* | *Target: > 15%* |

#### Order Profile

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Orders per day (average) | *e.g., 3.8* |
| Peak delivery hours | *e.g., 12:00–14:00, 18:00–20:00* |
| Most ordered items (top 3) | *e.g., Flat white, avocado toast, croissant* |
| Average items per order | *e.g., 2.3* |
| Refund/complaint rate (%) | *e.g., 4.2%* |
| Average customer rating | *e.g., 4.6/5.0* |

#### Operational Impact

| Factor | Rating (1–5) | Notes |
|--------|-------------|-------|
| Impact on in-store service during peak | *3* | *Delivery orders create bottleneck at bar 12:00–13:00* |
| Packaging complexity and time | *2* | *Simple — mostly drinks + pastries* |
| Driver wait time (average min) | *5* | *Drivers wait 3–5 min, manageable* |
| Order accuracy (delivery-specific errors) | *4* | *Low error rate* |
| Platform support responsiveness | *3* | *Slow on dispute resolution* |

**Rating:** 1 = Significant negative impact, 5 = No impact or positive

### Section 3: Direct Ordering Channel (if applicable)

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Direct orders (audit period) | *e.g., 45* | *Via website/phone* |
| Gross revenue (EUR) | *e.g., 630* | |
| Commission / processing fees (%) | *e.g., 3% (payment processing only)* | |
| Net revenue (EUR) | *e.g., 611* | |
| Net margin (%) | *e.g., 42%* | *Significantly higher than platforms* |
| Customer repeat rate (%) | *e.g., 35%* | |
| Marketing cost to drive direct orders (EUR) | *e.g., 50/month* | |

### Section 4: Platform Comparison Matrix

| Metric | Wolt | Bolt Food | Direct | Target |
|--------|------|-----------|--------|--------|
| Orders per month | *113* | *78* | *15* | |
| Avg order value (EUR) | *12.00* | *10.50* | *14.00* | > EUR 10 |
| Commission rate (%) | *30%* | *25%* | *3%* | < 25% |
| Net margin (%) | *23.8%* | *28.1%* | *42%* | > 15% |
| Customer rating | *4.6* | *4.4* | *N/A* | > 4.5 |
| Complaint rate (%) | *4.2%* | *6.1%* | *1.5%* | < 5% |
| Operational impact (1–5) | *3* | *3* | *5* | > 3 |
| **Strategic verdict** | *Keep, renegotiate commission* | *Review — high complaints* | *Invest in growth* | |

### Section 5: Strategic Decisions

| Decision | Action | Owner | Deadline |
|----------|--------|-------|----------|
| *Renegotiate Wolt commission* | *Contact account manager, target 25%* | *Owner* | *End of month* |
| *Investigate Bolt Food complaints* | *Review last 10 complaints — packaging or kitchen issue?* | *Manager* | *This week* |
| *Grow direct ordering* | *Add QR code to all in-store receipts linking to order page* | *Owner* | *Next month* |
| *Review delivery menu* | *Remove low-margin items, add delivery-exclusive bundles* | *Manager + Owner* | *2 weeks* |

### Section 6: Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Owner | | | |
| Manager (if co-reviewing) | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Not Accounting for All Costs

**Why it happens:** Owners look at commission percentage but forget packaging, labour, tablet rental, marketing fees, and refund costs.
**How to prevent:** Use the full financial breakdown in Section 2. Include every cost line, even small ones. They add up.
**If it's already happening:** Re-run Section 2 with all costs included. The real net margin is often 10–15 percentage points lower than expected.

### Risk 2: Staying on a Platform Out of Habit

**Why it happens:** "We've always been on Wolt" or "everyone uses it." No periodic review of whether it is actually profitable.
**How to prevent:** Run this audit quarterly. If a platform's net margin is below 10%, it needs either renegotiation or removal.
**If it's already happening:** Calculate your net margin right now. If it is below 10%, contact the platform for commission reduction before the next contract period.

### Risk 3: Neglecting Direct Ordering

**Why it happens:** Platforms are easy — they bring customers to you. Building a direct channel requires effort and marketing investment.
**How to prevent:** Even a simple Google Form or phone order system at 3% processing cost produces dramatically higher margins than any platform. Start small.
**If it's already happening:** Add a flyer in every delivery bag promoting direct ordering with a 10% discount code. Measure the conversion.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Consider whether delivery aligns with your brand. Some specialty cafes deliberately avoid delivery to protect drink quality. If you do deliver, curate a delivery-specific menu.
**Chain / Franchise:** Platform agreements may be negotiated at chain level. Use this audit to provide local data to head office for renegotiations.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Delivery may not apply. If operating from a fixed kiosk with delivery, focus on a single platform to keep operations simple.
**Bakery Cafe:** Baked goods travel well — delivery can be highly profitable. Track fragile item damage rates separately.

**Making it yours:**
- Add your specific platform names and login details (keep secure)
- Include screenshots of platform dashboard data for the audit file
- Track month-over-month trends, not just quarterly snapshots
- Add a row for "customer acquisition cost" if you use platform marketing/promotions

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T103-02 — Delivery Channel P&L Spreadsheet (L2, dashboard)
- T103-03 — Delivery Menu Margin Calculator (L2, template)
- T103-04 — Commission Negotiation Preparation Brief (L3, template)

**Related blocks:**
- B101
- B102

**Parent block:** B103 — Delivery Channel Strategy
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 — OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
